articles.title: War in the Middle East made the case for renewables - what’s happening in each country tells a harder story
articles.description: The oil-dependent world is in crisis. Ship traffic in the Strait of Hormuz – through which more than a quarter of global seaborne oil trade and a fifth of the world’s liquefied natural gas flow – is at a virtual standstill. Oil prices have climbed, briefly topping US$119 a barrel. The largest release of oil from countries’ strategic reserves in history ... Read more

articles.title: E85 fuel costs less than petrol, but here’s the fuel-efficiency reality
articles.description: News News: India has officially entered the E85 fuel era. Recently, the country’s first E85 fuel dispenser was inaugurated in Delhi, with the fuel priced at Rs 8.

articles.title: RBI Rate Cut Cycle Nears: Axis Direct Foresees June Policy Pivot
articles.description: The current market triggers such heightened oil prices, and a weak rupee among other factors are expected to prompt the regulator to reverse the rate cycle in the upcoming policy meetings, according to Axis Direct.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs sees FY27 as best year for FMCG growth since FY23, bets on consumer staples
articles.description: Arnab Mitra, India Consumer Analyst at Goldman Sachs, said consumer staples are likely to outperform small-ticket discretionary categories as inflation pressures household budgets. The brokerage also expects larger FMCG companies to gain market share and benefit from operating leverage despite concerns over crude oil prices and monsoon conditions.

articles.title: Oil Market Uncertainty Casts a Long Shadow
articles.description: The article discusses ongoing oil supply disruptions due to heightened tensions between the U.S., Iran, and Israel, causing fluctuating oil prices. Despite the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the market remains calm due to various unknowns. China’s oil consumption dynamics add to the uncertainty, affecting global supply-demand balance.

articles.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests Viyash Scientific, Federal Bank shares to buy in near-term
articles.description: The Indian stock market saw heavy losses, with the BSE Sensex dropping 1.11% and Nifty 50 declining 1.22%, driven by geopolitical tensions and a global tech selloff. Rising crude oil prices raised inflation concerns, but broader indices displayed relative resilience.

articles.title: Turbulent Markets: Middle East Tensions and AI Selloff Rattle Europe
articles.description: The European STOXX 600 index fell to a two-week low amid Middle East tensions and an AI stocks selloff. Airline stocks struggled with rising oil prices, while tech shares declined across global markets. Monte dei Paschi rose after a takeover bid by Intesa Sanpaolo, while investors eye the ECB's upcoming rate decision.

articles.title: South African rand weakens as oil rises on geopolitical jitters
articles.description:
South Africa's rand weakened in early trade on Monday as rising oil prices amid renewed Middle East tensions weighed on global risk appetite, putting pressure on the currency.

articles.title: Escalating Tensions: Israel Strikes Iranian Petrochemical Plant Amid Ongoing Conflict
articles.description: Israel launched a strike on Iran's Mahshahr petrochemical complex after U.S. president Trump urged restraint to pursue peace talks with Tehran. The ongoing conflict saw Yemen's Houthis threaten maritime navigation and both nations escalate attacks, raising oil prices by 3%. A ceasefire deal remains elusive.
